key_changes_for_sciences: Paper 3 completely removed from grade boundaries
Paper 1 split into Paper 1A and Paper 1B
Component breakdowns updated to reflect new structure

grade_boundaries.explanation: Grade boundaries vary slightly each session based on exam difficulty. These are May 2025 official boundaries or typical ranges based on recent trends. Boundaries are for the final scaled total out of 100 (combining all papers + IA).

notes.ia_weighting: Internal Assessment typically worth 20% of final grade
notes.paper_3: Only exists for History, Business, Geography, ITGS, Math AA HL. REMOVED for Sciences.

HL.post_2025_note: Paper 3 removed. Paper 1 split into 1A (MCQ) and 1B (Data analysis). Weightings adjusted.

SL.post_2025_note: Paper 3 removed. Paper 1 split into 1A (MCQ) and 1B (Data analysis). Weightings adjusted.

HL.post_2025_note: Paper 3 removed. Paper 1 split into 1A (MCQ) and 1B (Data analysis). Weightings adjusted.

SL.post_2025_note: Paper 3 removed. Paper 1 split into 1A (MCQ) and 1B (Data analysis). Weightings adjusted.

HL.post_2025_note: Paper 3 removed. Paper 1 split into 1A (MCQ) and 1B (Data analysis). Weightings adjusted.

SL.post_2025_note: Paper 3 removed. Paper 1 split into 1A (MCQ) and 1B (Data analysis). Weightings adjusted.

descriptors.0: Does not meet standards described below
descriptors.1: Little understanding of surface meaning; seldom supports claims with evidence
descriptors.2: Some understanding of surface meaning; evidence sometimes appropriate
descriptors.3: Understanding of surface meaning and some inferential understanding; evidence mainly relevant
descriptors.4: Detailed understanding of surface meaning and convincing inferential understanding; evidence relevant
descriptors.5: Detailed and insightful understanding; convincing and nuanced inferential understanding; well-selected evidence

criteria.what_is_assessed: An understanding of the text – what is revealed and inferred – using supporting references.

descriptors.0: Does not meet standards described below
descriptors.1: Little analysis of language/style; commentary is descriptive
descriptors.2: Some analysis of language/style; commentary mainly descriptive
descriptors.3: Mainly appropriate analysis of how language/style constructs meaning; some insight
descriptors.4: Appropriate analysis; some evaluation of how meaning is shaped by writer's choices
descriptors.5: Insightful and convincing analysis; very good evaluation of how meaning is shaped by writer's choices

criteria.what_is_assessed: An understanding of language, style, and structure, and an ability to critically evaluate writer's choices to construct meaning.

descriptors.0: Does not meet standards described below
descriptors.1: Little organization and no obvious focus
descriptors.2: Some organization and a degree of focus
descriptors.3: Adequate organization, some coherence, and some focus
descriptors.4: Good organization, good degree of coherence, adequate focus
descriptors.5: Effective organization and coherence; good degree of focus

criteria.what_is_assessed: An ability to organize ideas in a coherent and focused way.

descriptors.0: Does not meet standards described below
descriptors.1: Little clarity or accuracy; little sense of appropriate register
descriptors.2: Some clarity and accuracy; some sense of appropriate register; errors apparent
descriptors.3: Adequate clarity and accuracy; register mainly appropriate; some lapses
descriptors.4: Good clarity and accuracy; register consistently appropriate
descriptors.5: Very good clarity and accuracy; strong sense of precision; register consistently appropriate and effective

criteria.what_is_assessed: An ability to write with clarity, accuracy, and variety. An ability to write in an appropriate academic register. An ability to include relevant terminology where appropriate.

descriptors.0: Does not meet standards described below
descriptors.1-2: Little knowledge and understanding of works; little comparison in relation to question
descriptors.3-4: Limited knowledge and understanding; limited comparison in relation to question
descriptors.5-6: Satisfactory knowledge and understanding; satisfactory comparison in relation to question
descriptors.7-8: Good knowledge and understanding; relevant comparison; discussion somewhat sustained
descriptors.9-10: Very good knowledge and understanding; insightful comparison; discussion sustained and insightful

criteria.what_is_assessed: A knowledge and understanding of literary works. A response to the question that is relevant, showing similarities and differences in the works studied.

descriptors.0: Does not meet standards described below
descriptors.3-4: Somewhat analytical but mainly descriptive; limited comparison of writer's choices
descriptors.5-6: Generally analytical; some understanding of language/style/technique; some comparison of writer's choices
descriptors.7-8: Analytical and evaluative; good understanding; good comparison of writer's choices
descriptors.9-10: Analytical and evaluative; insightful and convincing understanding; very good comparison of writer's choices

criteria.what_is_assessed: An analysis and evaluation of language, technique, and style to establish meaning and effect. Through analysis and evaluation, appropriate similarities and differences are shown in response to the question.

descriptors.0: Does not meet standards described below
descriptors.2: Some focus and connection between ideas; coherence may be inconsistent
descriptors.3: Mainly good focus despite lapses; reasonable balance; general sense of cohesion
descriptors.4: Good focus largely maintained; good balance; ideas develop consistently and logically
descriptors.5: Clear and consistent focus; very good balance; ideas develop in logical and compelling way

descriptors.0: Does not meet standards described below
descriptors.1: Little clarity or accuracy; little sense of appropriate register
descriptors.2: Some clarity and accuracy; some sense of appropriate register; errors apparent
descriptors.3: Adequate clarity and accuracy; register mainly appropriate; some lapses
descriptors.4: Good clarity and accuracy; register consistently appropriate
descriptors.5: Very good clarity and accuracy; strong sense of precision; register consistently appropriate and effective

criteria.what_is_assessed: An ability to write with clarity, accuracy, and variety. An ability to write in an appropriate academic register. An ability to include relevant terminology where appropriate.

question_1.description: Short answer questions based on a single source

question_2.description: Analysis of origin, purpose, value, and limitation

question_3.description: Comparing two sources for similarities and differences

question_4.description: Extended response synthesizing all sources and own knowledge

marking_notes.question_1: Award 2 marks for each sub-part. Look for accurate identification of key points from source.
marking_notes.question_2: Award marks for OPVL analysis (Origin, Purpose, Value, Limitation). Higher marks require evaluation of source usefulness for a specific historical inquiry.
marking_notes.question_3: Award marks for comparing perspectives across sources. Look for explicit comparison language and analysis of differing viewpoints.
marking_notes.question_4: Award marks for essay structure, use of all sources, integration of own knowledge, and argument development. 8-mark response requires balanced argument and conclusion.

paper_2.title: Paper 2: World History Topics

marking_bands.0-2: Little understanding of question; lacks relevant knowledge; weak structure
marking_bands.3-5: Some understanding but limited; some relevant knowledge; basic structure
marking_bands.6-9: Adequate understanding; relevant knowledge; clear structure
marking_bands.10-12: Good understanding; detailed knowledge; good structure and analysis
marking_bands.13-15: Excellent understanding; precise knowledge; sophisticated analysis and evaluation

paper_2.marking_notes: Essays are marked holistically. Look for: clear thesis statement, detailed evidence from works studied, analysis of historical concepts (causation, change, continuity, significance, perspectives), and evaluation of different interpretations. Each essay worth 15 marks.

paper_3.title: Paper 3: HL Regional Options

marking_bands.0-2: Little understanding of question; lacks relevant knowledge
marking_bands.3-6: Some understanding; limited knowledge; basic structure
marking_bands.7-11: Adequate understanding; relevant knowledge; clear structure
marking_bands.12-15: Good understanding; detailed knowledge; good analysis and evaluation

paper_3.marking_notes: HL essays require deeper analysis and evaluation than Paper 2. Look for: sophisticated argument, integration of historiography, evaluation of different historical interpretations, detailed evidence, and critical thinking. Each essay worth 15 marks.

paper_1A.title: Paper 1A: Multiple Choice
paper_1A.marking: 1 mark per correct answer. No penalty for incorrect answers.
paper_1A.notes: Questions test knowledge, understanding, and application of concepts across the core syllabus. SL: 30 questions (45 min), HL: 40 questions (60 min).

paper_1B.title: Paper 1B: Data Analysis and Experimental Design
paper_1B.marking_approach: Award marks for accurate data interpretation, correct application of experimental design principles, clear explanations, and appropriate use of scientific terminology. Written answers required - NOT multiple choice.

section.description: Data analysis and experimental design questions (4-6 questions)
section.marking: Award marks for: graph interpretation, variable identification, error analysis, method evaluation, conclusion drawing.

skills_assessed: Error and uncertainty analysis (random, systematic, precision, accuracy)
paper_1B.post_2025_note: NEW section replacing old Paper 3 experimental skills. Requires written answers, not multiple choice.

paper_2.title: Paper 2: Short Answer and Extended Response
paper_2.marking_approach: Award marks for: accurate knowledge recall, correct application of concepts, clear explanations, structured arguments, and appropriate use of scientific terminology.

section_a.marking: Award marks for specific correct points. Typically 6-10 questions.

section_b.marking: Award marks for: depth of explanation, use of evidence, quality of reasoning, and structured argument. SL: choose 2 of 3, HL: choose 3 of 4.

command_term_marks.Discuss/Evaluate: 4-6 marks for balanced argument with evidence
command_term_marks.Calculate: 1-3 marks for correct formula, substitution, and answer

paper_3.note: Paper 3 has been completely removed in the 2025 syllabus. Option topics no longer exist. Experimental skills previously in Paper 3 Section A are now assessed in Paper 1B.

paper_1_and_2.title: Papers 1 & 2: Short and Extended Response
paper_1_and_2.marking_approach: Method marks awarded for correct working even if final answer is incorrect. Answer marks awarded for correct final answer.

section_a.marking: Award marks for correct method and final answer. Each question typically has 2-4 sub-parts.

section_b.marking: Award marks for: method, reasoning, accuracy, and communication. Each question typically has 3-5 sub-parts.

paper_1_and_2.marking_notes: Always award follow-through marks (FT) if an error in part (a) is carried forward to (b). Show all working clearly in mark scheme. Award marks for correct use of mathematical notation and logical flow.

paper_3_hl.title: Paper 3: Extended Problem Solving (HL only)
paper_3_hl.marking_approach: Each question has 5-7 sub-parts increasing in difficulty. Award marks for mathematical reasoning, proof construction, and problem-solving strategies.
paper_3_hl.marking_notes: Look for: logical flow, justification of steps, appropriate use of mathematical notation, complete reasoning even if final answer is partially incorrect. Questions require deeper mathematical thinking and often connect multiple syllabus areas.

Sciences_post2025.title: Internal Assessment: Scientific Investigation
Sciences_post2025.group_rule: Students can work in groups with similar methods, but independent variables must differ

criteria.description: Evidence of independent thinking, creativity, or initiative. Justification of topic choice showing personal significance.

descriptors.2: Clear evidence of personal engagement, independent thinking, and initiative

criteria.description: Background knowledge, research question, methodology, safety/environmental considerations.

descriptors.1-2: Limited background, unclear question, inappropriate method
descriptors.3-4: Adequate background, clear question, appropriate method
descriptors.5-6: Well-developed background, focused question, well-designed method

criteria.description: Data processing, appropriate presentation, uncertainty/error analysis.

descriptors.1-2: Limited processing, basic presentation, no error analysis
descriptors.3-4: Appropriate processing, clear presentation, some error analysis
descriptors.5-6: Thorough processing, effective presentation, detailed error analysis

criteria.description: Conclusion with justification, evaluation of method, suggestions for improvement.

descriptors.3-4: Clear conclusion with justification, reasonable evaluation
descriptors.5-6: Well-justified conclusion, thorough evaluation, realistic improvements

criteria.description: Clear presentation, appropriate terminology, logical structure.

Sciences_post2025.post_2025_note: IA now has 3000 character limit (not 3000 words). Group work permitted with different independent variables.

criteria.description: Organization, coherence, conciseness, and use of appropriate mathematical conventions.
Use of notation, terminology, logical reasoning, and clarity of explanations.
Independent thinking, creativity, curiosity, and personal connection to the topic.
Review of findings, limitations, implications, and evaluation of the exploration process.
Accuracy, depth of understanding, sophistication of mathematics used, and appropriate level for the course.
Critical analysis of two sources (one primary, one secondary) using OPVL. Evaluation of origins, purpose, values, and limitations in relation to the investigation.
Analysis of evidence, argument development, balanced conclusion. Must demonstrate understanding of historical concepts (causation, change, continuity, significance, perspectives).
Reflection on methods used and challenges faced by historians. Discussion of limitations of the investigation and how a historian might approach the topic differently.
